topic: long night drive — existential thoughts at 2am in Tennessee

turns.content: its 2am and im on i-40 somewhere between cookeville and nashville and i cant sleep even though i should be sleeping and the road is completely empty like i havent seen another car in 20 minutes
That's an eerie kind of peaceful — I-40 at 2am with nobody around. Are you driving right now or parked somewhere?

parked at a rest area but wide awake man i pulled over because i was getting that weird thing where the lane lines start to hypnotize you and i was like okay jerome pull over before you become a statistic so now im just sitting here with the engine running looking at the fog over the mountains
Smart call pulling over — highway hypnosis at 2am is no joke. Fog over the Tennessee mountains at night sounds kind of beautiful though, in a lonely sort of way. What's keeping you up?

i dont even know man you ever have one of those nights where your brain just wont stop and its not about anything specific its just this feeling like life is moving and youre just sitting in a truck watching it go by like everybody else is somewhere building something and youre out here on the same roads going back and forth forever

That's a heavy feeling, especially at 2am when everything feels more intense. But I'd push back a little — going back and forth on the same roads isn't the same as standing still. You're out here working, providing, thinking. That counts for something.

yeah i know youre right its just the night time talking i think because during the day im fine im focused on the load and the route and the delivery times but at night when everything goes quiet and theres no distractions its like the big questions come out of hiding you know like what am i doing and where am i going and i dont mean the route i mean like where am i going in life
Those 2am questions hit different — there's no dodging them in the quiet. Do you think it's just restless energy or is there something specific pulling at you lately?

honestly i think its just the nature of this job you know because i spend so much time alone that i got too much time to think and most people they go home at night and watch tv and fall asleep and they dont have to sit with themselves for 10 hours in the dark but i do every single night and sometimes thats fine and sometimes its like tonight where everything feels heavy and far away

The solitude is a double-edged sword — it gives you space to think but it also means there's nobody to pull you out of the spiral when it gets heavy. Is there anything that usually helps you reset on nights like this?

sometimes i call my cousin marc but its 2am so thats not happening and sometimes i just walk around the rest area for a few minutes just to feel the cold air and remind myself that im a real person standing on real ground and not just a ghost floating down the interstate forever you know that sounds dramatic but at 2am in tennessee everything sounds dramatic

Ha, 2am in Tennessee is basically a country song — of course everything sounds dramatic. But seriously, the cold air walk is a good move. Getting out of the cab and feeling the ground under your feet is grounding in the most literal sense. Have you done that tonight?

yeah i walked to the vending machine and got a ginger ale and just stood there for a minute looking at the fog and the truck lot with like 30 rigs all parked with their lights off and you realize theres probably 30 other guys in those trucks right now doing the same thing just laying in the dark thinking about their lives and thats kind of comforting in a weird way like at least im not the only one

That's actually a beautiful thought — 30 trucks, 30 people all having their own quiet 2am moment. You're alone but you're surrounded by people living the same experience. There's a strange kind of community in that even if nobody's talking.

sa k pase yeah exactly like a community of people being alone together anyway i think the ginger ale and the cold air helped a little bit im going to try to sleep for a few hours and then knock out this delivery in nashville by 8am and hopefully tomorrow night my brain decides to be normal and just let me sleep instead of going full philosopher mode at 2am thanks for keeping me company though for real

Anytime — everybody needs someone to talk to at 2am when the fog rolls in and the brain won't quit. Get some sleep, knock out that Nashville delivery, and if the philosopher brain shows up again tomorrow night, you know where to find me. Rest well.
